policies.headline: Banks in strong position, NPA may drop to 1.9% by March 2027
policies.summary: RBI's Financial Stability Report states that Indian banks' gross NPA ratio fell to 2.1% by September 2025, a multi-decade low. Under normal conditions, it may further decline to 1.9% by March 2027. Banks have adequate capital and liquidity buffers.

policies.headline: Indian economy on path of high growth: RBI Governor
policies.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra wrote in the Financial Stability Report that despite adverse external factors, Indian economy is expected to register high growth on the strength of strong domestic consumption and investment.

policies.headline: Stablecoins pose threat to monetary sovereignty: RBI
policies.summary: RBI has warned that widespread use of stablecoins can pose a serious threat to the country's monetary sovereignty and financial stability. Stablecoins are digital currencies pegged to stable assets like the dollar or gold.

policies.headline: Small savings scheme interest rates unchanged for Q4
policies.summary: Central government kept interest rates on PPF and other small savings schemes unchanged for the seventh consecutive quarter. Rates effective from January 1 to March 31, 2026.

policies.headline: EU carbon tax effective today, setback for Indian exporters
policies.summary: EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) becomes effective from January 1, 2026, impacting India's aluminium and steel exporters. Indian exporters may need to cut prices by 15-22% to remain competitive. Small exporters may be pushed out of the EU market.

policies.headline: Safeguard duty on steel products is a balanced decision: Nandal Jindal
policies.summary: Indian Steel Association chairman Naveen Jindal lauded the government's decision to impose safeguard duty on select steel products for three years, with 12% duty in the first year, to protect domestic steel industry from cheap imports.

policies.headline: Fiscal deficit reaches 62.3% of budget estimate by November
policies.summary: Central fiscal deficit reached Rs 9.76 lakh crore or 62.3% of the annual budget target by November 2025, compared to 52.5% in the same period last year. Total spending was Rs 29.25 lakh crore (57.8% of budget). Total income was Rs 19.49 lakh crore.

policies.headline: GST revenue rises 6.1% to Rs 1.74 lakh crore in December, domestic collection pace slows
policies.summary: Gross GST collection in December 2025 rose 6.1% YoY to Rs 1,74,550 crore. However, domestic revenue growth slowed after GST rate cuts. Import-related GST surged 19.7% to Rs 51,977 crore. Net GST collection after refunds was Rs 1,45,570 crore. Maharashtra led with Rs 9,852 crore.

policies.headline: Insurance premiums rising due to high-cost distribution strategies: RBI
policies.summary: RBI has flagged concerns about insurance premiums being driven by high-cost distribution strategies rather than operational efficiency. Life insurance sector's high acquisition costs are a persistent concern. Total premium income was Rs 11.9 lakh crore in 2024-25 vs Rs 8.3 lakh crore in 2020-21.

policies.headline: Tobacco companies required to install CCTV on packaging machines, retain footage for 2 years
policies.summary: From February 1, 2026, tobacco, zarda and gutka manufacturers must install CCTV on all pouch-packing machines and retain footage for at least 24 months. GST and capacity-based cess will create a comprehensive tax framework to curb tax evasion.

policies.headline: Rs 2,000 notes: 98.41% returned to banks
policies.summary: 98.41% of Rs 2,000 denomination notes have been returned to banks. Only Rs 5,669 crore worth notes remain with the public. RBI had announced withdrawal on May 19, 2023.

policies.headline: Banks can now set up own pension funds for NPS management
policies.summary: PFRDA approved reforms allowing scheduled commercial banks to set up their own pension funds and manage NPS. Banks managing over $177 billion in assets will now be able to participate more directly, subject to RBI eligibility norms.

policies.headline: Electronic manufacturing: 22 new proposals approved, Rs 41,863 crore investment expected
policies.summary: Government approved 22 new proposals under the Electronic Components Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS), expecting Rs 41,863 crore investment and Rs 2,58,152 crore production value. Expected to create 33,791 jobs. Key companies include Foxconn, Dixon, Samsung, Tata Electronics and Hindalco. Manufacturing covers 11 product categories including PCBs, capacitors, connectors and lithium-ion cells.

policies.headline: Textile sector PLI application deadline extended to March 31, 2026
policies.summary: Government extended the last date for new applications under the textile sector PLI scheme to March 31, 2026, from the earlier deadline of December 31.

policies.headline: Manufacturing PMI drops to 2-year low of 55 in December
policies.summary: India's HSBC manufacturing PMI fell to 55 in December 2025, the lowest in 2 years, from 56.6 in November. New export orders saw the weakest growth in 14 months. Production growth was the slowest in 38 months. However, price inflation dropped to a 9-month low.

policies.headline: Rs 7,295 crore export support package announced for exporters
policies.summary: Government announced Rs 7,295 crore export support package including Rs 5,181 crore interest subsidy and Rs 2,114 crore credit guarantee support for MSME exporters. Subsidy of up to 2.75% per annum for eligible MSME exporters. Part of the larger Rs 25,060 crore Export Promotion Mission.

policies.headline: Corporate Ministry orders probe against Shree Cement
policies.summary: Ministry of Corporate Affairs ordered an investigation against cement manufacturer Shree Cement and sought information from the Bangur family-promoted company. The probe is under Section 210(1)(c) of the Companies Act 2013.

policies.headline: Corporate bonds offer better returns than bank FDs for retail investors
policies.summary: Special feature on corporate bonds as an investment alternative for retail investors. SEBI's 2024 reforms reduced minimum investment from Rs 10 lakh to Rs 10,000. Top corporate bond funds offering 7.37-8.44% returns vs bank FDs at 6.25-7.50%. Bond market opened up for common investors after decades of being restricted to institutions.

policies.headline: EPF withdrawal rules simplified into three categories
policies.summary: EPFO has simplified its withdrawal rules by reducing 13 categories to just 3: mandatory requirements, housing needs, and special circumstances. Full withdrawal allowed at age 58/retirement, VRS, permanent disability, or emigration. Partial withdrawals for housing (after 5 years), medical emergency, marriage/education (after 7 years).

policies.headline: RBI drafts new rules for foreign exchange transactions transparency
policies.summary: RBI has drafted new rules for foreign exchange transactions requiring authorized dealers to show complete cost breakdown before transactions. Banks must display all charges and rates clearly. Rules aim to make retail foreign payments more transparent.

policies.headline: India overtakes China to become world's largest rice producer
policies.summary: India's rice production reached 15.18 crore tonnes, surpassing China's 14.5 crore tonnes to become the world's largest rice producer. Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan released 184 new crop varieties across 25 crops. Modi government approved 3,236 high-yield varieties since 2014.

policies.headline: Tata Group begins search for new Air India CEO, Wilson to be replaced
policies.summary: Tata Group has begun searching for a new CEO for Air India, planning to replace current chief Campbell Wilson before his term ends in 2027. Chairman N Chandrasekaran is reportedly dissatisfied with slow reforms and ground-level progress. Fatal June crash and subsequent safety issues prompted the move.

policies.headline: CCI finds 25 steel companies guilty of price-fixing, heavy fines likely
policies.summary: Competition Commission of India found 25 steel companies including JSW Steel, Tata Steel, SAIL and their 56 top officials guilty of collusive price-fixing from 2015-2023. Companies face fines up to 10% of turnover per year of violation. JSW Steel has 17.5% market share, Tata Steel 13.3%, SAIL 10%. Case originated from a 2021 builder complaint about 55% price hike in just 6 months.

policies.headline: Sensex falls 376 points for second consecutive day on tariff fears
policies.summary: Sensex fell 376.28 points to 85,063.34 and Nifty dropped 71.60 points to 26,178.70 for the second straight session. Reliance Industries crashed 4.42%, losing nearly Rs 1 lakh crore in market cap - the biggest single-day fall since June 4, 2024. FII sold Rs 10,763 crore while DII bought Rs 1,749.35 crore. BSE market cap fell Rs 1.20 lakh crore to Rs 479.60 lakh crore.

policies.headline: Silver surges Rs 7,000, gold up Rs 1,100 as fourth consecutive session of gains
policies.summary: Silver jumped Rs 7,000 to Rs 2,51,000 per kg, its third consecutive day of sharp gains. Gold rose Rs 1,100 to Rs 1,41,500 per 10 grams, its fourth straight session of gains. Strong global signals and industrial demand drove the rally. International silver at $31.75/oz.

policies.headline: RBI likely to cut repo rate by 0.50% this year too
policies.summary: RBI has room for another 0.50% repo rate cut in 2026, according to IIFL Capital report. In 2025, rates were cut by 1.25%. The gap between repo rate and retail inflation currently stands at 2.8%, suggesting additional easing room.

policies.headline: Services PMI drops to 11-month low of 58 in December
policies.summary: HSBC India services PMI fell to 58 in December 2025 from 59.8 in November, the slowest expansion since January 2025. New business and output growth slowed. Employment growth hit a near 3.5-year low. Companies reported demand from Asia, North America, West Asia and Britain.

policies.headline: India GDP growth likely to be 6.9% in FY27: India Ratings
policies.summary: India Ratings projects 6.9% GDP growth for FY2026-27. GST and income tax cuts, reforms and trade agreements will drive growth. Retail inflation expected to average 3.8%. A low-tariff India-US trade deal could further boost GDP.

policies.headline: SEBI proposes allowing stock price data for educational purposes with 30-day delay
policies.summary: SEBI proposed allowing use of listed companies' stock price data for educational purposes with a 30-day delay. The proposal aims to help investor education while protecting market integrity. Comments invited till January 27.

policies.headline: India's GDP to grow at fastest pace of 7.4%
policies.summary: NSO released first advance estimate of national income showing India's GDP will grow at 7.4% in FY2025-26, higher than RBI and other agencies' estimates. Nominal GDP expected at Rs 357.14 lakh crore. Manufacturing sector to grow at 7% and services at 9.1%.

policies.headline: SEBI extends incentive period for mutual fund distributors
policies.summary: SEBI extended the deadline for additional incentives to mutual fund distributors for adding new individual investors from B-30 cities and new women investors from any city to March 1, 2026, from the earlier February 1 deadline.

policies.headline: Eternal receives Rs 3.7 crore GST notice
policies.summary: Eternal (formerly Zomato) received a GST demand notice for Rs 3,69,80,242 including interest and penalty for the period April 2019 to March 2020 from West Bengal Additional State Tax Commissioner (Appeals).

policies.headline: Rupee strengthens 31 paise against dollar
policies.summary: Indian rupee strengthened 31 paise against the US dollar to close at 89.87, supported by falling crude oil prices and RBI intervention. The rupee opened at 90.20 and traded in the 89.75-90.23 range.

policies.headline: Markets fall for 4th straight session, Rs 7.7 lakh crore wiped out
policies.summary: Sensex fell 780.18 points (0.92%) to 84,180.96 and Nifty dropped 263.90 points (1.01%) to 25,876.85 - biggest fall since Aug 26, 2025. Metal, oil-gas and commodity stocks saw heavy selling. FIIs sold Rs 3,367.12 crore. Investors lost Rs 7.68 lakh crore in market cap across four sessions.

policies.headline: RBI to support tariff-affected exporters with new measures
policies.summary: RBI is considering new ways to support exporters affected by US tariffs, as few took advantage of existing loan repayment moratorium. The central bank is in talks with export agencies and may ease eligibility criteria or issue concessional-rate loans.

policies.headline: Eternal receives Rs 27.5 crore GST notice
policies.summary: Eternal received two GST notices totaling Rs 27.56 crore including interest and penalty from West Bengal Additional Commissioner of State Tax (Appeals) for the period April 2020 to March 2022 related to output tax underpayment.

policies.headline: SEBI amends stockbroker regulations for ease of compliance
policies.summary: SEBI amended three-decade-old stockbroker rules to allow brokers to operate activities under other financial regulators' frameworks. The move aims to simplify language, ease compliance and make business operations simpler.

policies.headline: UIDAI launches Uday mascot for Aadhaar services
policies.summary: UIDAI launched a mascot named 'Uday' to make Aadhaar-related communications more user-friendly. The mascot will help explain updates, authentication, offline verification, and new technology usage in simple language.

policies.headline: Heavy tobacco tax will render small retailers unemployed
policies.summary: Federation of Retailer Association of India warned that massive tax hikes on tobacco products from Feb 1 will render small shopkeepers unemployed and boost illegal sales. FRAI represents 80 lakh small, medium and large retailers.

policies.headline: Stalled 61,000 homes being completed with SWAMIH fund
policies.summary: Government's SWAMIH stress fund completed construction of over 61,000 stalled homes across 30 cities by Dec 15. The fund aims to provide relief to 4 lakh+ homebuyers, including 7,000+ EWS and rehabilitation category units. The fund committed its entire investment corpus.

policies.headline: Sensex falls for 5th straight session, Rs 13.49 lakh crore wealth eroded
policies.summary: Sensex fell 604.72 points to 83,576.24 and Nifty dropped 193.55 points to 25,683.30 on Friday, marking fifth consecutive session of decline. Total wealth erosion over five sessions was Rs 13.49 lakh crore. FIIs sold Rs 3,769.31 crore while DIIs bought Rs 5,595.84 crore.

policies.headline: Vodafone Idea gets 15 years to pay AGR dues
policies.summary: Vodafone Idea got major relief from the government with 15 years (until FY2041) to pay Rs 87,695 crore AGR dues. The company will pay Rs 1,144 crore annually for the first 10 years and Rs 124 crore for next 6 years, then Rs 100 crore for 4 years. Government holds 48.9% stake in VIL.

policies.headline: No need for rate cut with strong growth and controlled inflation: PwC
policies.summary: PwC Partner Ranen Banerjee said there's no need to cut interest rates given strong growth and controlled inflation. He compared unnecessary rate cuts to 'wasting a bullet.' Private capital investment is stalled due to demand uncertainty, not interest rates. RBI MPC meeting scheduled Feb 4-6.

policies.headline: India's forex reserves fall $9.8 billion in a week
policies.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves fell $9.80 billion to $686.80 billion in the week ending Jan 2, as RBI sold dollars to defend the rupee. Foreign currency assets dropped $7.62 billion. Gold reserves fell $2.05 billion to $111.26 billion.

policies.headline: Fake GST notices alert: CBIC warns taxpayers
policies.summary: CBIC flagged concerns over fake GST notices being sent to businesses. Fraudsters are copying official GST documents, using fake CGST logos and fake DIN numbers. CBIC urged taxpayers to immediately report receiving such fraudulent notices.

policies.headline: SEBI amends trading system framework for technical glitches
policies.summary: SEBI revised the framework for dealing with technical glitches in electronic trading systems. Compliance rules simplified, small brokers exempted. Glitches beyond broker control no longer penalized. Framework applies only to brokers with 10,000+ registered clients.

policies.headline: Five companies get SEBI nod for IPOs
policies.summary: OEMI Technology, Indo MIM and three others received SEBI approval to launch IPOs. Additionally, Kusumgar, Elcoqui Distilleries and Earth Spatiates also got approval. These five companies can raise over Rs 3,000 crore through IPOs.

policies.headline: China emerges as top export destination for India at 4-year high
policies.summary: India's exports to China surged to a 4-year high in the April-November period of FY2025-26, signaling structural shift in bilateral trade relations. Key export items include oil cake, marine products, telecom equipment and spices. Electronic segment exports, especially printed circuit boards, also rose significantly.

policies.headline: Indian investors sending record money abroad for property and global stocks
policies.summary: Indian investors sent over $2 billion abroad in 9 months (Jan-Sep 2025) under LRS scheme, up 55% vs 2024. Real estate investment abroad rose 80% to $35 million. Equity and debt instrument investment rose 50%+ to $1.68 billion. GIFT City provides tax-free route for global investing.

policies.headline: Cash transaction limits and rules every taxpayer should know
policies.summary: Comprehensive guide on cash transaction limits under Indian tax law: Rs 20,000+ cash prohibited for property deals (Section 269SS), Rs 2 lakh limit for jewelry and luxury items, Rs 20,000+ cash loans to friends prohibited, and bank account transactions monitored via SFT.

policies.headline: Wrong UPI transfer: steps for recovery
policies.summary: Guide on recovering money sent to wrong UPI ID: file immediate complaint with transaction ID, contact bank's customer care, request receiver to return money, file complaint on NPCI portal, and approach RBI Banking Ombudsman as last resort.

policies.headline: Direct tax collection up 9% to Rs 18.38 lakh crore
policies.summary: Net direct tax collection grew 8.82% to Rs 18.38 lakh crore up to Jan 11. Gross collection rose 4.14% to Rs 21.50 lakh crore. Corporate tax collection exceeded Rs 8.63 lakh crore. Government targets Rs 25.20 lakh crore for FY2025-26, 12.7% higher than previous year. Refunds dropped 17% to Rs 3.12 lakh crore.

policies.headline: Food prices up but retail inflation still below 2% for fourth consecutive month
policies.summary: CPI retail inflation rose to 1.33% in December 2025, a three-month high, but remained below RBI's 2% lower comfort level for the fourth consecutive month. Food inflation improved to -2.71% from -3.91% in November. Urban inflation crossed 2% to 2.03%. Personal care segment saw highest inflation at 28.07%.

policies.headline: Cotton import duty removal demanded by textile industry
policies.summary: Indian Cotton Industry Federation demanded permanent removal of 11% import duty on cotton from Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan. This would help reduce cost pressure on domestic companies.

policies.headline: Construction equipment sales drop 9% in April-December
policies.summary: Domestic construction equipment sales fell 9% to 81,566 units in April-December 2025 due to slowdown in infrastructure activities and project completion difficulties. However, exports rose to 12,469 units from 9,733 units.

policies.headline: Government finalizing rare earth mineral permanent magnet manufacturing scheme
policies.summary: Heavy Industries Minister H D Kumaraswamy said the government is finalizing the Request for Proposal (RFP) for the rare earth mineral permanent magnet manufacturing scheme. He invited domestic and international companies to participate in the bidding process.

policies.headline: GST 2.0 drives auto industry to record 44.89 lakh passenger vehicle sales
policies.summary: Indian passenger vehicle wholesale sales hit a record 44.89 lakh+ units in 2025, up 5% from 2024's 42.74 lakh. Utility vehicles led with 7% growth to 29.54 lakh units. Two-wheeler sales crossed 2 crore for the first time. December 2025 saw 26.6% YoY growth in PV sales to 3,99,216 units. GST rate cuts, income tax relief and repo rate cuts fueled the growth.

policies.headline: NPS to get guaranteed pension scheme
policies.summary: Government has started preparations to provide guaranteed pension under NPS similar to UPS. PFRDA formed a 15-member high-level committee chaired by former IBBI Chairman Dr. M.S. Sahoo to develop framework and rules for market-based guarantees and subscriber protection.

policies.headline: Electronic exports cross Rs 4 lakh crore in 2025
policies.summary: India's electronic exports exceeded Rs 4 lakh crore in 2025 driven by government's manufacturing push. Minister Ashwini Vaishnav said four semiconductor plants will begin production in 2026, further boosting exports. iPhone exports from India reached Rs 2.03 lakh crore in 2025. Mobile phone production could reach 30 billion units by end of FY2025-26.

policies.headline: India slips to 3rd in Russian oil buyers as Reliance cuts imports
policies.summary: India became the third-largest buyer of Russian fossil fuels in December 2025 as Reliance Industries and government refineries sharply cut Russian crude imports. Russia's share in India's total oil imports fell 10 percentage points to 25% in December. Turkey overtook India at 2nd position with 2.6 billion euros. China remained top buyer.

policies.headline: Central government capex may slow in remaining months: Morgan Stanley
policies.summary: Morgan Stanley warns central government may slow capital expenditure in remaining months of FY2025-26 as 58.7% of budgeted Rs 11.21 lakh crore has already been spent in April-November. Capital spending reached Rs 6.6 lakh crore. Roads and railways account for 55% of capex. State capex remains at 1.7% of GDP.

policies.headline: RBI proposes resuming urban cooperative bank licensing
policies.summary: RBI has proposed resuming the licensing process for urban cooperative banks (UCBs) after a two-decade hiatus since 2004. The process will be subject to regulatory requirements including minimum capital of Rs 300 crore.

policies.headline: Wholesale inflation hits 8-month high due to surge in food prices
policies.summary: WPI inflation rose to 0.83% in December 2025, the highest in 8 months. Food items, manufactured products and non-food articles drove the increase. Calendar year 2025 average WPI was only 0.6% vs 1.74% in 2024.

policies.headline: SMBC gets approval to set up wholly-owned subsidiary in India
policies.summary: RBI has given in-principle approval to Japan's Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation to establish a wholly-owned subsidiary in India. SMBC has acquired 24.22% stake in Yes Bank.

policies.headline: Record Rs 14.25 billion equity investment in Indian real estate in 2025
policies.summary: Indian real estate attracted record $14.25 billion equity investment in 2025, up 25% YoY from $11.43 billion. Land/development dominated at 46% of total investment. CBRE reports strong institutional interest.

policies.headline: SEBI settles with Blue Coast Hotels for Rs 89 lakh
policies.summary: Blue Coast Hotels and its promoter settled with SEBI for Rs 89.3 lakh over alleged disclosure norm violations found in financial statements from 2018-19 to 2021-22.

policies.headline: India becoming world's second largest factory but foreign brands benefiting more from PLI
policies.summary: India has become world's largest consumer market and second largest factory. Mobile production surged 28x from Rs 18,000 crore to Rs 5.45 lakh crore in a decade. However, foreign brands like Samsung and Asus benefit more from PLI and government tenders than domestic companies.

policies.headline: Bajaj General Insurance launches fetal health insurance policy
policies.summary: Bajaj General Insurance launched 'Fetal Flourish' policy covering 16 prenatal procedures for women aged 18-45. Premium starts at Rs 1,025. Includes Rs 2 lakh rider for two maternity events.

policies.headline: NSE settlement petition accepted by SEBI; IPO likely soon
policies.summary: SEBI has in-principle agreed to NSE's settlement petition in the co-location unfair market access case. NSE offered Rs 1,388 crore payment in 2025. This paves the way for NSE's long-pending IPO. Large companies can sell 2.5% stake in IPOs.

policies.headline: Postal exports to get export incentive scheme benefits from Jan 15
policies.summary: Government extended RoDTEP and RoSCTL export incentive schemes to goods exported via postal channels from January 15, 2026. This will boost MSME exporters especially in small towns. CBIC amended regulations to enable duty drawback claims for postal exports.

policies.headline: Tech Mahindra reports Rs 1,122 crore profit in Q3
policies.summary: Tech Mahindra Q3 net profit was Rs 1,122 crore, up 14.11% YoY. Revenue rose to Rs 14,393 crore from Rs 13,286 crore. Higher tax provisions due to new labor laws increased costs. Headcount fell by 872.

policies.headline: Nutraceuticals: The rising global empire of health supplements
policies.summary: Global nutraceuticals market exceeds $350 billion with 6% annual growth. India targets developing a $100 billion nutraceuticals market by 2035. US accounts for 25-30% of global market. Regulations vary significantly across countries.

policies.headline: SEBI proposes easier KYC registration for investors
policies.summary: SEBI proposed measures to simplify customer identification and KYC registration processes. Centralization of supplementary information during KRA-level registration will reduce repeated data submission across intermediaries.

policies.headline: Budget day February 1 to be a trading day despite being Sunday
policies.summary: BSE and NSE will remain open for trading on February 1 (Sunday) as Union Budget is being presented that day. Normal trading session from 9.15 AM to 3.30 PM.

policies.headline: TCS refund on car purchase: How to claim back 1% tax collected at source
policies.summary: Guide explaining how buyers of cars worth over Rs 10 lakh can claim back the 1% TCS (Tax Collected at Source) through income tax return filing. The amount is adjusted against tax liability or refunded within 2-3 months.

policies.headline: Tax saving tips on selling residential property
policies.summary: Guide on capital gains tax when selling residential property. Long-term gains (after 24 months) taxed at 12.50%. Tax can be saved by reinvesting in new house or bonds from NHAI, REC, PFC, IRFC.

policies.headline: Cyber insurance: Essential protection against growing online fraud
policies.summary: With over 26.5 crore cyber attacks in 2025 (7 lakh+ daily), personal cyber insurance has become essential. Article covers types of cyber crimes, insurance benefits, policy selection tips, and coverage details.

policies.headline: Government approves export of 5 lakh tonnes of wheat flour
policies.summary: Government allowed export of 5 lakh tonnes of wheat flour and related products after over 3 years of partial ban. Wheat export ban was imposed in 2022. Applications to be accepted from Jan 21 to Jan 31, with monthly rolling window until quota is exhausted.

policies.headline: First Global Economic Cooperation summit to be held Feb 17-19 in Mumbai
policies.summary: The first Global Economic Cooperation summit will be held February 17-19 in Mumbai, organized by Future Economic Cooperation Council. Policymakers, global CEOs and investors will discuss economic diplomacy and capital coordination.

policies.headline: Manufacturing PMI at quarterly high; 91% companies report stable/higher output
policies.summary: FICCI manufacturing survey shows the index at a quarterly high in Q3 with 91% companies reporting stable or higher output vs 87% in Q2. Average capacity utilization is around 75%. However, production costs remain elevated due to raw material prices and rupee weakness.

policies.headline: PhonePe gets SEBI approval for IPO
policies.summary: SEBI has approved PhonePe's IPO. The issue will be an Offer for Sale by existing shareholders. PhonePe dominates UPI with 45%+ market share and completed 9.8 billion transactions in December. FY25 revenue was Rs 7,115 crore.

policies.headline: RBI report: Only 35% policyholders keep life insurance till maturity; 7.5% payout for death claims
policies.summary: RBI's Financial Stability Report reveals only 35% of policyholders keep life insurance till maturity. 37% of total payouts were for pre-maturity surrenders. Death claims accounted for only 7.5% of payouts. Total life insurance payouts rose from Rs 4 lakh crore in FY21 to Rs 6.3 lakh crore in FY25.

policies.headline: MSME quality certification fee exemption to be extended by 3 years
policies.summary: Government plans to extend quality certification fee exemption for MSMEs by 3 years. Micro enterprises to get 80% discount, small enterprises 50%, and medium 20% on BIS certification. BIS has published 23,000+ standards, 95% aligned with international standards.

policies.headline: RBI bulletin: India's GDP position inspires hope, reforms will boost infrastructure
policies.summary: RBI January bulletin states India's current economic position inspires optimism despite global risks. GDP growth estimate for FY26 shows India remaining fastest-growing major economy. Total financial resources flow to commercial sector reached Rs 30.8 lakh crore in Apr-Dec 2025. FDI inflows showed annual improvement.

policies.headline: NCLT extends protection for IL&FS directors appointed by government
policies.summary: NCLT extended protection period for professional directors of IL&FS new board against intentional defaulter designation by banks. Government had appointed the board on October 1, 2018.

policies.headline: Rural workers' retail inflation rises in December
policies.summary: December retail inflation for agricultural and rural workers rose 0.04% and 0.11% respectively compared to November. Food inflation was -1.80% for farm workers and -1.73% for rural workers.

policies.headline: BHIM UPI app introduces auto top-up feature for low balance
policies.summary: NPCI's BHIM app introduced auto top-up feature - when UPI balance drops below Rs 200, it auto-recharges. BHIM app monthly transactions grew from 3.89 crore to 16.5 crore in December. NPCI MD Lalita Nagaraj announced partnerships with Canara Bank and plans for more PSU banks.

policies.headline: Discom losses to reduce with cost-reflective tariff provision in electricity amendment bill
policies.summary: Government plans to introduce cost-reflective tariff concept in Electricity Amendment Bill to reduce distribution company losses. Minister Manohar Lal said BTOC discoms' complaints will be addressed first. Bill expected to be discussed in budget session.

policies.headline: Budget 2026 to focus on aggressive reforms, infrastructure spending and exports
policies.summary: Economist Sandeep Vempati expects Union Budget 2026-27 to focus on ease of business, exports, employment, manufacturing and infrastructure. Budget size expected to be Rs 55-56 lakh crore. Capital expenditure may rise to Rs 12.25-12.5 lakh crore.

policies.headline: Pesticide industry demands stricter e-commerce rules for crop protection products
policies.summary: CropLife India demanded stricter regulations for online sale of crop protection products. They want provisions in the Pesticide Management Bill to regulate e-commerce platforms selling agricultural inputs.

policies.headline: Government may double infra spending to Rs 3 lakh crore in Budget
policies.summary: Government could double infrastructure spending to Rs 3 lakh crore in Budget 2026-27. Focus on highways, urban transport and smart cities. Logistics Sector Skill Council CEO Ravikant Yarthiesh said India needs to invest in modern warehousing, cold-chain facilities and retail logistics.

policies.headline: Postal Department targets 30% revenue growth this fiscal
policies.summary: Department of Posts targets 30% revenue growth to Rs 17,546 crore in FY2025-26. Five of six departments showed revenue growth. Previous year total revenue was Rs 13,240 crore. Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia announced the target.

policies.headline: Startups urge government not to use Supreme Court tax ruling for scrutinizing old investments
policies.summary: 60 startups appealed to government not to use Supreme Court's Mauritius tax ruling to investigate pre-2017 investments. The court had found Carlisle and Advent involved in tax evasion via Flipkart deal. Tiger Global denied wrongdoing. Startup Policy Forum CEO warned of negative impact on FDI.

policies.headline: Razorpay POS gets RBI license as offline payment aggregator
policies.summary: Razorpay POS received RBI license as offline payment aggregator. The company now holds all three major RBI licenses: online payment aggregator, cross-border payment aggregator, and offline payment aggregator.

policies.headline: SEBI charges PwC and EY with insider trading in Yes Bank share sale case
policies.summary: SEBI charged PwC, EY, Carlisle and Advent officials with insider trading in Yes Bank's 2022 share offering. 19 people including senior officials face charges. Advisors allegedly shared unpublished price-sensitive information. SEBI asked EY chairman Rajiv Memani to explain why penalties shouldn't be imposed.

policies.headline: RBI to inject over Rs 2 lakh crore liquidity into banking system
policies.summary: RBI announced injection of over Rs 2 lakh crore into banking system through OMO bond purchases of Rs 1 lakh crore (Rs 50,000 crore each on Feb 5 and 12) after reviewing current liquidity and financial conditions.

policies.headline: Five companies to invest Rs 863 crore under AC components PLI scheme
policies.summary: Five companies selected under PLI scheme for AC components will invest Rs 863 crore. Total production target is Rs 8,337 crore with 1,799 additional direct jobs by FY2027-28. The scheme offers 4-6% incentive on sales growth.

policies.headline: Government releases discussion paper on new GDP series with 2022-23 base year
policies.summary: Ministry of Statistics released discussion paper on new GDP quarterly series and sub-national accounts computation. The new national accounts series will have FY2022-23 as base year.

policies.headline: EPF withdrawal now possible via UPI from April 2026
policies.summary: EPFO members will be able to withdraw provident fund directly via UPI from April 2026. Rs 25,000 per transaction limit set. BHIM app will enable direct connection. 75% of total balance can be withdrawn; 25% remains as minimum balance. Education withdrawals increased to 10 times.

policies.headline: RBI launches unified ombudsman scheme 2026 for faster complaint resolution
policies.summary: RBI issued Integrated Ombudsman Scheme 2026 effective July 2026 for faster resolution of banking customer complaints. No limit on dispute amount. RBI ombudsman can award up to Rs 30 lakh compensation and Rs 3 lakh for harassment/time wastage.

policies.headline: Government targets 3-nanometer chip manufacturing by 2032
policies.summary: IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnav announced India's target to manufacture 3nm chips by 2032 under Design Linked Incentive scheme Phase 2, focusing on six categories: compute, radio frequency, networking, power, sensor and memory chips.

policies.headline: Adani-Embraer partnership to build passenger aircraft in India
policies.summary: Adani Group and Brazilian aircraft maker Embraer signed MoU to manufacture passenger aircraft in India. Partnership covers aircraft assembly, supply chain, MRO, sales and pilot training. India's domestic aviation market is world's third largest with need for 2,000 aircraft in next 20 years.

policies.headline: Budget 2026: Aggressive reforms focus on defense, health, education, women empowerment
policies.summary: Pre-budget analysis: FM Sitharaman to present record 9th budget focusing on reforms. Health budget may cross Rs 1.25 lakh crore. Education budget could reach Rs 1.50 lakh crore. Defense budget expected to see 20% increase. India Development & Strategic Fund for green energy and MSMEs. FDI rose 73% to $47 billion.

policies.headline: Markets rally for second straight day; Sensex up 487 points, Rs 5.85 lakh crore added
policies.summary: Sensex surged 487.20 points to 82,344.68 crossing 82,000. Nifty gained 167.35 points to 25,342.75. Market cap rose Rs 5.85 lakh crore to Rs 459.53 lakh crore. BEL rose 8.90%, Eternal 5.09%. FIIs sold Rs 480 crore while DIIs bought Rs 3,361 crore.

policies.headline: Industrial production hits 2-year high of 7.8% growth in December
policies.summary: India's Industrial Production Index (IIP) grew 7.8% in December 2025, a two-year high, driven by mining, manufacturing and power sectors. Manufacturing grew 8.1% vs 3.7% a year ago. Mining output reached 6.8% from 2.7%. Power generation rose to 6.3%.

policies.headline: Economic Survey: Developed nations' protectionism signals end of globalization; Swadeshi push essential
policies.summary: Economic Survey 2025-26 warns that export controls, technology denial and carbon tax regimes signal end of globalization. India must pursue import substitution and strategic self-reliance. Swadeshi is both a defensive and offensive policy tool. Survey calls for disciplined approach to managing external shocks.

policies.headline: Economic Survey: Poverty reduced significantly, bottom 5-10% saw highest spending growth
policies.summary: Economic Survey shows government welfare measures significantly reduced poverty. Bottom 5-10% of population saw highest per-capita monthly expenditure growth in both rural and urban areas between 2022-23 and 2023-24. Subsidies, pensions and direct benefit transfers helped the weakest sections.

policies.headline: MSME sector faces Rs 8.1 lakh crore pending payments challenge
policies.summary: Economic Survey highlights Rs 8.1 lakh crore in pending payments to MSMEs as a major barrier to growth. Need for cash-flow based lending and digital integration. Despite MSME credit growth, limited documentation and guarantees remain hurdles for micro enterprises.

policies.headline: Economic Survey suggests redefining public sector companies at 26% government holding
policies.summary: Economic Survey suggests redefining public sector companies - currently at 51% government holding, the threshold could be reduced to 26%. This would allow government to sell stake below 51% while maintaining influence. It would ease privatization and boost government revenue.

policies.headline: Insurance distribution commissions remain high concern despite digitization
policies.summary: Economic Survey flags high distributor commissions on insurance products as a barrier to penetration despite digitization. Large share of policyholder premiums goes to distribution commissions, making insurance expensive. Digital-first approach needed to reduce costs.

policies.headline: Skill development: Only 4.9% of youth aged 15-29 have formal vocational training
policies.summary: Economic Survey notes only 4.9% of 15-29 age group has formal vocational training while 21.2% got informal training. Investing in formal skill development could increase employment by 13% in labor-intensive sectors by 2030. District skill committees need strengthening.

policies.headline: NSE gets SEBI green light for Rs 10,000 crore IPO after decade-long wait
policies.summary: SEBI granted NOC to National Stock Exchange for its IPO after over a decade. NSE had first filed for a Rs 10,000 crore share sale in 2016 but was delayed due to governance and co-location issues. SEBI chairman recently gave in-principle approval to NSE's settlement petition.

policies.headline: US dollar falls to four-year low amid tariff policy uncertainty
policies.summary: US dollar fell to four-year low against euro and pound, dropping 3% in one week. Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs, Greenland tensions and shifting investor sentiment drove the decline. Dollar index fell 10% last year, worst since 2017.

policies.headline: Fiscal deficit at 54.5% of budget target by December; government revenue at Rs 25.25 lakh crore
policies.summary: Central government fiscal deficit reached Rs 8.55 lakh crore by December 2025, or 54.5% of FY2025-26 budget target of Rs 15.69 lakh crore. Total government revenue was Rs 25.25 lakh crore. Tax revenue was Rs 19.39 lakh crore. Non-tax revenue was Rs 5.39 lakh crore. Total expenditure was Rs 25.93 lakh crore.

articles.headline: India's GDP to grow at fastest pace of 7.4%
articles.summary: NSO released first advance estimate of national income showing India's GDP will grow at 7.4% in FY2025-26, higher than RBI and other agencies' estimates. Nominal GDP expected at Rs 357.14 lakh crore.

articles.headline: Housing sales in top 8 cities drop to 3.48 lakh units

articles.headline: Union Bank profit rises to Rs 5,073 crore in Q3
articles.summary: Union Bank net profit rose 9.7% to Rs 5,073 crore in Q3. Deposit growth rate was 3.36%, below banking system average. Bank found Rs 4,200 crore gap between existing and expected credit loss provisions

articles.headline: China's 5% GDP growth target under threat due to real estate crisis
articles.summary: China's 5% GDP growth target is under threat as real estate crisis and traditional sector weakness offset high-tech gains. Rhodium Group says new industries added only 0.8 points to growth. Property s

articles.headline: IMF raises India's growth forecast; to remain fastest growing major economy
articles.summary: IMF raised India's FY26 GDP growth estimate to 7.3% from 6.6%, a 0.7 percentage point increase. FY27 and FY28 growth projected at 6.4% each. India to remain world's fastest growing major economy for a

articles.headline: Moodys: India's 7.3% GDP growth to boost insurance demand
articles.summary: Moody's projects India's GDP growth at 7.3% for FY26 and says strong economic expansion will boost insurance demand. Total insurance premium grew 17% to Rs 10.9 lakh crore in Apr-Nov. Health insurance

articles.summary: Ministry of Statistics released discussion paper on new GDP quarterly series and sub-national accounts computation. The new national accounts series will have FY2022-23 as base year.

articles.summary: Wood Mackenzie VP Joshua Ngu said India needs $145 billion annual energy investment to bridge gap between economic growth and carbon emission reduction. Focus should be on power generation, energy sto

articles.headline: Manufacturing PMI drops to 2-year low of 55 in December
articles.summary: India's HSBC manufacturing PMI fell to 55 in December 2025, the lowest in 2 years, from 56.6 in November. New export orders saw the weakest growth in 14 months. Production growth was the slowest in 38

articles.headline: RBI likely to cut repo rate by 0.50% this year too
articles.summary: RBI has room for another 0.50% repo rate cut in 2026, according to IIFL Capital report. In 2025, rates were cut by 1.25%. The gap between repo rate and retail inflation currently stands at 2.8%, sugge

articles.headline: FMCG sector volume growth expected to rise 5%, recovery underway
articles.summary: FMCG sector volumes expected to grow 5% in coming months, driven by strong economic indicators, low inflation and negative food inflation. Consumer confidence is returning. FMCG posted 5.3% growth in

articles.headline: India GDP growth likely to be 6.9% in FY27: India Ratings
articles.summary: India Ratings projects 6.9% GDP growth for FY2026-27. GST and income tax cuts, reforms and trade agreements will drive growth. Retail inflation expected to average 3.8%. A low-tariff India-US trade de

articles.summary: PwC Partner Ranen Banerjee said there's no need to cut interest rates given strong growth and controlled inflation. He compared unnecessary rate cuts to 'wasting a bullet.' Private capital investment

key_insights: Silver surged 60.88% in January 2026, outperforming gold (up 22.73%) by 38.2 percentage points — the widest precious metals divergence in years.
DIIs invested Rs 57,482.42 crore across the month to cushion FII outflows of Rs 21,537.64 crore, with a net DII-to-FII absorption ratio of 2.7x.
The Sensex declined 3.46% in January (from 85,220.6 to 82,269.8), with 12 losing days versus just 8 winning days out of 20 trading sessions.
The Indian rupee depreciated Rs 2.05 against the dollar (from 89.88 to 91.93), hitting a record low of 91.99 during the month despite forex reserves crossing $709 billion.
Gold prices swung wildly — peaking at Rs 183,000 per 10g on Jan 30 before crashing Rs 14,000 in a single day on Jan 31, marking the biggest single-day gold correction in domestic markets.
Silver recorded a jaw-dropping Rs 40,500 single-day surge on January 28, reaching Rs 404,500/kg before correcting — an unprecedented move in Indian commodities history.
FIIs sold a massive Rs 36,353.44 crore across 11 selling sessions, with the single biggest outflow of Rs 10,763 crore on January 7.
Corporate news dominated media coverage with 43 articles (12.5%), followed by banking & finance (37 articles) and government policy (30 articles).

Overall news sentiment was mildly positive at 0.2166, with technology/telecom (0.51) and investment advisory (0.45) being the most optimistic categories, while currency/forex (-0.17) was the most negative.
Thursday was the only consistently positive weekday for the Sensex (avg +124 pts, 60% win rate), while Wednesday was the worst (avg -343 pts, 25% win rate).
Budget 2026 anticipation drove a surge in government policy coverage in week 4 (13 articles vs 3 in week 3), with the Economic Survey released on January 30.
India's GDP growth outlook remained strong with multiple upgrades — IMF, UN, and NSO projections ranged between 6.6% to 7.4%, making India the fastest-growing major economy.
The gold-silver ratio declined from 0.5762 to 0.4395, indicating silver's massive outperformance and possible supply-side squeeze in industrial metals.
Global economy coverage surged 400% from the first half to the second half of the month, reflecting mounting concerns over Trump tariffs, EU trade tensions, and the Venezuela crisis.
SIP investments hit a record Rs 31,002 crore in December while mutual fund AUM added Rs 14 lakh crore in 2025, underscoring the growing strength of domestic retail participation.
The FII-DII divergence persisted on 12 of 17 tracked days — whenever FIIs sold, DIIs stepped in to buy, creating a remarkably consistent counter-flow pattern.
narrative_summary:
January 2026 was a month of stark contrasts in India's financial landscape. The equity markets opened the year on a hopeful note, with the Sensex gaining 573 points on January 3, but the optimism proved short-lived as persistent FII selling, Trump tariff anxieties, and global uncertainties dragged the benchmark down 3.46% by month's end, closing at 82,269.78. The Nifty mirrored this trajectory, declining 3.1% to 25,320.65. Of the 20 trading sessions, 12 ended in losses, with January 21 marking the worst day as the Sensex plunged 1,065.71 points.

The most dramatic story, however, belonged to precious metals. Silver staged an extraordinary rally, surging 60.9% during the month from Rs 2,39,000 to Rs 4,04,500 per kilogram at its peak. A single-day jump of Rs 40,500 on January 28 was unprecedented in Indian commodities history. Gold also rallied strongly, gaining 22.7% and touching Rs 1,83,000 per 10 grams on January 30 before a sharp Rs 14,000 correction on the last day of the month. The gold-silver ratio declined sharply from 0.576 to 0.440, suggesting a supply-side squeeze in silver markets potentially linked to Venezuelan geopolitical tensions and safe-haven demand.

The institutional flow dynamic was a defining theme. Foreign Institutional Investors pulled out a net Rs 21,538 crore, selling on 11 of 17 tracked days, with a single-day outflow of Rs 10,763 crore on January 7 — one of the largest single-day exits in recent memory. Domestic Institutional Investors provided a critical counterbalance, investing Rs 57,482 crore across the month, buying on 15 of 16 tracked days. This 2.7x absorption ratio demonstrated the structural maturity of India's domestic capital markets, underpinned by record SIP flows (Rs 31,002 crore in December) and mutual fund AUM growth of Rs 14 lakh crore in 2025.

The Indian rupee continued its depreciating trend, weakening Rs 2.05 against the dollar from 89.88 to 91.93, breaching the psychologically important 90 level and hitting record lows multiple times. However, India's foreign exchange reserves provided comfort, crossing an all-time high of $709.4 billion by month's end.

On the macroeconomic front, India's growth narrative remained compelling. The IMF, UN, and NSO each projected GDP growth between 6.6% and 7.4%, making India the world's fastest-growing major economy. Retail inflation remained below 2% for the fourth consecutive month, though wholesale inflation hit an 8-month high driven by food prices. India's FDI surged 73% in 2025, and the country overtook China as the world's largest rice producer.

The month culminated in Union Budget 2026 anticipation and the Economic Survey release on January 30. The Survey highlighted the need for Swadeshi (self-reliant) manufacturing amid rising global protectionism. Policy activity intensified in week 4 with RBI announcing a Rs 2 lakh crore liquidity injection, SEBI acting on insider trading cases, and multiple Free Trade Agreement developments with the EU, Chile, and the UK. Corporate earnings were mixed — IT majors TCS, HCL, and Infosys reported profit declines due to new labor code costs, while banking sector NPAs continued their downward trajectory toward a projected 1.9% by March 2027. The 344 articles extracted from Amar Ujala's Karobar section across 26 daily editions painted a picture of an economy navigating significant global headwinds while maintaining strong domestic fundamentals.
